steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is "democratic strength, if associated with inequality, restrains inflationary pressures." This conditional phrasing indicates that democratic institutions only reduce inflation in the context of inequality (e.g., as in Norway) — contradicting the earlier speculation that democracy itself inhibits inflation control.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice C directly states that treating the Norway-Cameroon inflation gap as evidence of democratic institutions' shortcoming is "a mistake," which matches the conclusion that democratic strength can help control inflation when inequality is present.

A/B/D either support the disproven speculation, question methodology, or dispute index accuracy — all inconsistent with the study's conditional finding.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
